title: TCP lifecycle, TIME_WAIT, and CLOSE_WAIT
description: TCP connections progress through handshake, established, and teardown states; CLOSE_WAIT signals the app forgot to close, TIME_WAIT is normal churn.
body: Use when interpreting socket state during a network problem. A TCP connection opens with a three-way handshake, serves data while ESTABLISHED, and tears down with FIN/ACK exchange. CLOSE_WAIT on the local side means the peer closed and the application has not yet called close, so many CLOSE_WAIT sockets indicate a leak in the app's close path. TIME_WAIT is the normal state after an active close, held briefly to absorb stray packets; a flood of it indicates high connection churn (no keep-alive). Do not raise FD limits to mask a CLOSE_WAIT leak. Actions: read socket states with ss/netstat and map them to app behavior. Validation signals include CLOSE_WAIT count near zero and connection reuse limiting TIME_WAIT. Risk boundary: a CLOSE_WAIT leak will exhaust descriptors and take down the process; fix the close path.
